skill.name: lint-typecheck
skill.description: Run ESLint and TypeScript checks for a frontend project, then summarize lint warnings, lint errors, and type errors.
skill.summary: Validate frontend code with ESLint and TypeScript before commit or review.
skill.author: AppleLamps

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive; no cryptographic algorithm is present there. The external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs Bash execution of npm and TypeScript commands, but the commands match the skill purpose and no malicious intent, network access, credential access, or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Project Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to run frontend linting and TypeScript commands through Bash. This is legitimate for a linting skill, but npm scripts execute project-defined code and should be treated as trusted-repository actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs Bash execution of npm and TypeScript commands. The context is legitimate quality checking, but external command execution remains a meaningful operational risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Finding
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3, but that line only contains the skill description. No evidence found for cryptographic code or weak cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter text describing when to run lint and type checks. It contains no cryptographic API, hash name, or security-sensitive implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Commands In Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains Bash command examples for linting, type checking, and optional lint fixing. These commands are expected for the skill, but they can execute repository-defined npm behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in the skill instructions. They are not obfuscated, and the stated purpose matches linting and type checking.

content.value_statement: Frontend changes can pass review while hiding lint warnings or TypeScript errors. This skill runs the expected checks and reports actionable results before commit.
actual_capabilities: Runs the frontend ESLint command from the frontend directory.
Runs the TypeScript build check without emitting output files.
Reports lint warnings, lint errors, and type errors as a summary.
Lists detected errors with file locations when checks fail.
Offers ESLint auto-fix only after user confirmation.
limitations: It assumes the repository has a frontend directory.
It depends on existing npm scripts and TypeScript configuration.
It does not modify TypeScript configuration to hide errors.
It does not replace project-specific test suites or runtime checks.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Frontend Validation
use_cases.description: Run lint and type checks before committing frontend changes.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Quality Review
use_cases.description: Check whether a branch has lint or type issues before review.

use_cases.title: Automated Fix Triage
use_cases.description: Separate auto-fixable lint issues from TypeScript type mismatches.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Run lint and type checking for the frontend, then summarize the results.

prompt_templates.title: Check Before Commit
prompt_templates.scenario: Routine validation before creating a commit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the frontend before commit. Report lint warnings, lint errors, and TypeScript errors.

prompt_templates.title: Review Failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the frontend checks and list every error with its file location and likely cause.

prompt_templates.title: Triage Fix Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for deciding safe fixes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run lint and type checks, identify auto-fixable ESLint issues, and ask before applying any fixes.

output_examples.output: The report lists the lint command result, any warnings or errors, and a short pass or fail summary.

output_examples.input: Check for type errors before I commit.
output_examples.output: The report identifies TypeScript failures by file location and explains the type mismatch in plain language.

output_examples.input: Can you fix the lint issues?
output_examples.output: The assistant first reports detected issues, then asks for confirmation before applying automatic ESLint fixes.

best_practices: Run the checks after changing frontend files and before opening a pull request.
Review reported file locations before applying automatic lint fixes.
Keep TypeScript configuration changes separate from routine lint fixes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to suppress TypeScript errors by changing configuration.
Do not apply lint fixes without reviewing the reported changes.
Do not treat lint and type checks as a replacement for tests.

faq.question: What does this skill run?
faq.answer: It runs ESLint and a TypeScript no-emit build check for the frontend project.

faq.question: Does it change files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only offers automatic ESLint fixes after user confirmation.

faq.question: Can it fix TypeScript errors?
faq.answer: It identifies type mismatches, but fixes depend on the surrounding code and user approval.

faq.question: Does it require a specific project layout?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions assume a frontend directory with npm and TypeScript tooling.

faq.question: Is it useful in Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that can run shell checks.

faq.question: What security caution should users know?
faq.answer: The skill runs local npm commands, so users should only run it in repositories they trust.
